info.title: Torii Backend API
info.description: Server-to-server API (BAPI) for backend SDKs. Authenticated with a secret key (Bearer sk_live_* / sk_test_*).

tags.name: Allowed Origins
tags.description: Escape-hatch origins for non-web stacks (secret key auth)

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Invite end-users into the environment (secret key auth)

tags.name: Server Users
tags.description: Server user management endpoints (secret key auth)

tags.name: Server Sessions
tags.description: Server session management endpoints (secret key auth)

get.summary: List escape-hatch origins for this environment

put.summary: Replace the escape-hatch origins for this environment
put.description: Full origins incl. non-http schemes (e.g. capacitor://localhost). Replaces the list.

post.description: Creates an end-user in your environment. All body fields are optional; supply at minimum an email if you want the user to be able to sign in via email + password.

400.description: Invalid body (e.g. weak password, malformed email).

409.description: Another user with this email already exists in the environment.

post.description: Reverses a previous ban. The user can sign in again on next request.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to unban.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

post.description: Creates a single-use, short-lived impersonation token for the target user, attributed to `actorUserId`. Redeem it via `POST /_torii/auth/session/impersonate` to obtain a session and access token as the target user. Counts against the same per-period impersonation quota and usage ledger as the dashboard.

200.description: The minted token and its lifetime.

400.description: Deleted target, or `expiresInSeconds` out of the 60..600 range.

402.description: Plan does not include impersonation, or the per-period quota is exhausted.

403.description: Target or actor is in another environment, or impersonation is disabled for this environment.

post.description: Marks the user as banned and revokes all their active sessions.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to ban.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

post.description: Returns a cursor-paginated page of end-users in the environment matching the optional filters. Uses POST so the filter body can be sent without URL-encoding. Three id-selectors resolve users to a set of ids (`userIds`, the explicit batch-by-id lookup; `emailAddresses`, exact and case-insensitive; `email`, a case-insensitive substring); when more than one is supplied they are combined with AND (intersection). The remaining filters (`name`, `statuses`, `createdAfter`/`createdBefore`) apply on top.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items in the returned page (default 20).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by the previous page's `nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

post.summary: Create an invitation with optional pre-seeded metadata
Resend a pending invitation with a fresh link

get.description: Returns the full profile for one end-user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to fetch.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the user. Not idempotent at the HTTP layer: the authorization grant for the user is revoked on the first successful delete, so a subsequent DELETE for the same id returns 403 rather than 204. Treat 403 from a retry as a confirmation that the user is already deleted.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to delete.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

patch.description: Partial update with tri-state PATCH semantics. Every field in `UpdateUserRequest` is tri-state: omit the key to leave the field unchanged, send a non-null value to set it, or send JSON null to clear it.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to update.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

patch.description: Deep-merges into any of the three metadata bags. Each bag is tri-state: omit the key to leave the bag unchanged, or send an object to deep-merge into the existing bag (a key set to null removes it). The merged metadata is capped at 8 KB total across `publicMetadata`, `privateMetadata`, and `unsafeMetadata` combined (no per-bag limit).

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user to update.

400.description: A bag exceeds its size limit.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

get.description: Returns all active (unexpired, unrevoked) sessions for the user, ordered by most recently used.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user whose sessions to list.

200.description: All active sessions for the user.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

delete.description: Immediately revokes every active session for the user. Idempotent.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user whose sessions to revoke.

403.description: User belongs to a different environment.

delete.description: Revokes a single session by id. Idempotent: returns 204 even if the session was already revoked or expired.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user who owns the session.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Identifier of the session to revoke.

403.description: User or session belongs to a different environment.

CreateUserRequest.description: Request body for creating an end-user in your environment. All fields are optional; supply at minimum an email if you want the user to be able to sign in via email + password.

email.description: Primary email for the new user. If omitted, the user is created without a sign-in identity.

password.description: Initial password. Subject to the environment's password policy. Omit to create a passwordless user (e.g. social-only).

firstName.description: First (given) name to seed on the profile.

lastName.description: Last (family) name to seed on the profile.

publicMetadata.description: Initial public metadata (SDK-readable, server-written). Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

privateMetadata.description: Initial private metadata (server-only). Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

unsafeMetadata.description: Initial unsafe metadata (end-user writable). Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

ServerUserResponse.description: An end-user, including server-only private metadata. Returned only on the secret-key backend API.

environmentId.description: Identifier of the environment this user belongs to.

name.description: Full name on the profile, if any.

firstName.description: First (given) name on the profile, if any.

lastName.description: Last (family) name on the profile, if any.

locale.description: Preferred locale for emails and UI messages.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the user (e.g. active, banned).

createdAt.description: When this user was created (ISO-8601 UTC).

updatedAt.description: When this user was last modified (ISO-8601 UTC).

email.description: Primary email on the profile, if any.

emailVerifiedAt.description: When this user's primary email was verified, if it has been verified.

deletedAt.description: When this user was deleted, if soft-deleted. Null for active users.

publicMetadata.description: Public metadata: readable by the SDK, writable only server-side.

privateMetadata.description: Private metadata: server-only. Never exposed to the SDK or in a JWT.

unsafeMetadata.description: Unsafe metadata: readable and writable by the end-user via the SDK.

ServerImpersonationTokenRequest.description: Body for minting an impersonation token.

actorUserId.description: The principal the impersonation is on behalf of (recorded for accountability). Must be a user in this environment.

reason.description: Mandatory justification (GDPR purpose limitation); recorded in the audit log on mint and redeem.

expiresInSeconds.description: Optional token lifetime in seconds, 60..600. Omit for the 60s default.

token.description: The single-use token. Redeem via POST /_torii/auth/session/impersonate.

expiresInSeconds.description: The token's lifetime in seconds (the resolved value after any override).

ServerUserSearchRequest.description: Optional filter body for `POST /users/search`. Every field is tri-state: omit to skip that filter, send a value to apply it. The three id-selectors (`userIds`, `emailAddresses`, `email`) resolve users to a set of ids and, when more than one is supplied, are combined with AND (intersection); a supplied id-selector whose resolved set is empty returns an empty page. `name` additionally accepts JSON null to match users with no name; an explicit null or blank `email` contributes no restriction; the non-nullable `statuses` field rejects null.

name.description: Filter by name (case-insensitive substring match). Send null to require users with no name.

userIds.description: Restrict to these user ids (the explicit batch-by-id lookup), at most 100. AND-combined with the other id-selectors; an empty list returns an empty page.

emailAddresses.description: Resolve users by exact (case-insensitive) email address (one or more, at most 100). Unlike `email`, never matches a superstring. AND-combined with the other id-selectors; an empty list, or addresses matching nobody, returns an empty page.

email.description: Filter by primary email (case-insensitive substring match). AND-combined with the other id-selectors. An explicit null or blank value contributes no restriction.

statuses.description: Filter by user status. Returns users matching any of the supplied statuses.

createdAfter.description: Only return users created at or after this instant (ISO-8601 UTC).

createdBefore.description: Only return users created at or before this instant (ISO-8601 UTC).

CursorPageResponseServerUserResponse.description: A single page of results in a cursor-paginated list. Pass `nextCursor` as the `cursor` query parameter to fetch the following page.

items.description: Items in this page, in stable order.

nextCursor.description: Cursor to pass to fetch the next page. Null when this is the last page.

hasMore.description: True if more pages are available (equivalent to `nextCursor != null`).

UpdateUserRequest.description: PATCH body for updating an end-user. Every field is tri-state: omit the key entirely to leave the field unchanged, send a non-null value to set it, or send JSON null to clear it.

firstName.description: New first (given) name. Send null to clear; omit to leave unchanged.

lastName.description: New last (family) name. Send null to clear; omit to leave unchanged.

locale.description: New preferred locale. Send null to clear; omit to leave unchanged.

unsafeMetadata.description: Deep-merges into the user's unsafe metadata (a key set to null removes it); omit to leave unchanged. Counts toward the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

UpdateUserMetadataRequest.description: PATCH body for a user's metadata bags. Each bag is tri-state: omit to leave it unchanged, or send an object value. Whether the object merges into or replaces the bag depends on the endpoint (see its operation description).

publicMetadata.description: Public metadata bag: SDK-readable, server-written. Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

privateMetadata.description: Private metadata bag: server-only, never exposed to the SDK or in a JWT. Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

unsafeMetadata.description: Unsafe metadata bag: readable and writable by the end-user via the SDK. Part of the 8 KB combined metadata budget.

UserSessionResponse.description: An active end-user session in your environment.

userId.description: Identifier of the end-user this session belongs to.

environmentId.description: Identifier of the environment this session belongs to.

userAgent.description: Raw User-Agent string captured when the session was created.
userAgent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_6_0) AppleWebKit/537.36

ipAddress.description: IP address captured when the session was created.

createdAt.description: When this session was created (ISO-8601 UTC).

lastUsedAt.description: When this session was last seen by the API (ISO-8601 UTC).

activeOrganizationId.description: Active organization pinned to this session (`org_id` claim on re-mint).

impersonatedBy.description: Platform user behind this session when it was established via impersonation; null for normal sign-ins.

CursorPageResponseEnvironmentInvitationR.description: A single page of results in a cursor-paginated list. Pass `nextCursor` as the `cursor` query parameter to fetch the following page.

items.description: Items in this page, in stable order.

nextCursor.description: Cursor to pass to fetch the next page. Null when this is the last page.

hasMore.description: True if more pages are available (equivalent to `nextCursor != null`).

bearerAuth.description: Backend secret key (`sk_live_*` / `sk_test_*`) sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.
